Name: Pattern Recognition - PE
Text: Context PostgreSQL database with claims adjusters and claim events Intended Flow R A E P N C Actual Model POWL Contains specific anomalies

Name: Pattern Recognition - PE
Text: loop OperatorPOWL operator Operator LOOP children E P Executes E then either exits the loop or does P then E again

Name: Backtracking - PE
Text: Wait the prompt says E evaluate then either exit the loop or execute P approve and then E again Standard loop semantics This means its possible to Evaluate Approve Evaluate Approve or Evaluate and exit without Approving Self correction I need to point out both the repeated loop and the possibility of exiting without approval

Name: Pattern Recognition - PE
Text: xor OperatorPOWL operator Operator XOR children N skip Notify customer or skip

Name: Pattern Recognition - PE
Text: root StrictPartialOrder nodes R A loop xor C Edges are R to A A to loop loop to xor A to C This means C can be executed right after A allowing premature closing

Name: Deductive Reasoning - PE
Text: Anomaly 1 The Loop Semantics Evaluation Approval Loop In loop semantics E is the do part and P is the redo part This means a claim can be Evaluated and then exit the loop without Approval Also it allows a cycle of Approve Evaluate Approve

Name: Deductive Reasoning - PE
Text: Anomaly 2 Skipping Notification The XOR between N and skip explicitly allows the process to bypass notifying the customer entirely

Name: Deductive Reasoning - PE
Text: Anomaly 3 Premature Closing Partial Order Flaw The graph has an edge A to C Because its a partial order and lacks an edge xor to C the constraint for C is only that A has occurred It does not require loop or xor to finish Thus a claim could be closed immediately after being assigned to an adjuster

Name: Hypothesis Generation - PE
Text: Hypothesis A Business Rule Change A new conditional approval or evaluation only path was added for low value claims where formal approval is not needed but the modeler forgot to separate it from the standard approval path

Name: Hypothesis Generation - PE
Text: Hypothesis B Rework Claims often fail initial evaluation and need to be sent back for more information but the model is forcing an Approval before a reevaluation which is logically backwards

Name: Hypothesis Generation - PE
Text: Hypothesis C System Limitation Miscommunication Marketing or IT implemented an opt out notification feature for privacy reasons but claims processing was not properly notified leading to a blanket skip option rather than a data driven one

Name: Hypothesis Generation - PE
Text: Hypothesis D Draft Testing The silent transition is a leftover from process model testing that was not removed before deployment

Name: Hypothesis Generation - PE
Text: Hypothesis E Short circuiting for Rejected Invalid Claims Business wanted a way to quickly close junk claims right after assignment without doing the full evaluation Instead of creating a specific Reject Early activity they just drew an edge to Close

Name: Hypothesis Generation - PE
Text: Hypothesis F Modeling Error The process modeler accidentally created an edge from A to C instead of xor to C and there was no validation tool to catch missing end state dependencies

Name: Deductive Reasoning - PE
Text: Goal 1 Find claims closed without proper evaluation approval Need to find claims with a C event but missing E or P events or where P happens after C or P is missing entirely

Name: Heuristic Reasoning - PE
Text: Query 1 Missing E or P entirely before C Use EXISTS for C and NOT EXISTS for E and P before the C timestamp

Name: Deductive Reasoning - PE
Text: Goal 2 Find claims with multiple approvals or the E P E P loop pattern Count occurrences of P per claim

Name: Heuristic Reasoning - PE
Text: Query 2 Multiple Approvals GROUP BY claim id HAVING COUNT greater than 1 WHERE activity equals P

Name: Deductive Reasoning - PE
Text: Goal 3 Find claims where notification was skipped Find closed claims that do not have an N event

Name: Heuristic Reasoning - PE
Text: Query 3 Skipped Notification EXISTS for C NOT EXISTS for N

Name: Deductive Reasoning - PE
Text: Goal 4 Find claims closed immediately after assignment Look at the time difference between A and C If C happens before any E or N and close to A

Name: Heuristic Reasoning - PE
Text: Query 4 Premature Close Timing Get the timestamp of A and C and ensure no E P N happened in between

Name: Validation - PE
Text: Review against Prompt Requirements Did I identify anomalies Yes Loop semantics XOR skip Partial order short circuit Did I generate hypotheses Yes Did I provide Postgres queries to verify Yes provided 4 distinct queries covering the specific anomalies

Name: Backtracking - PE
Text: Self correction during formatting Ensure the explanation of the loop operator is highly accurate This means exiting after E without P is entirely possible
